title: Pharmaceutical Industry on Pace for Record Lobbying Spending
excerpt: Pharma spent $227M lobbying in the first half of 2025, on pace to surpass its $388M record set in 2024, as the fight over TCJA tax break extensions intensified.

title: Warren Asks Defense Contractors to Explain Lobbying for Tax Breaks
excerpt: Sen. Warren pressed Northrop Grumman, Lockheed Martin, RTX, and General Dynamics on their lobbying to bring retroactive full expensing back into the tax code.

title: 7 Ways the Big Beautiful Bill Cuts Taxes for the Rich
excerpt: Analysis of how the tax bill delivers targeted breaks to favored industries, from bank real-estate lending exemptions to special oil and gas deductions.

title: Oil & Gas Industry: Background and PAC Contributions
excerpt: The fossil fuel industry spent over $150M lobbying the federal government in 2024, including for expansion of tax deductions for oil and gas exploration.

title: Real Estate PAC Contributions to Candidates, 2023-2024
excerpt: Real estate PACs gave $13.6M to federal candidates in the 2024 cycle, making them one of the largest industry PAC contributors focused on preserving the mortgage interest deduction.

title: Schakowsky, Warren Hit Big Pharma for Paying Zero Federal Taxes
excerpt: Five pharmaceutical giants — Abbvie, Pfizer, Amgen, Merck, and J&J — paid $0 in federal taxes while lobbying for even more tax breaks through the TCJA extension.

title: U.S. Spending Bill to Grant $40 Billion in Fossil Fuel Subsidies
excerpt: The reconciliation bill added nearly $40B in new federal subsidies for oil, gas, and coal, sending an additional $4B per year out the door for fossil fuels over the next decade.

title: How U.S. Tax Policy Subsidizes Oil and Gas Extraction Abroad
excerpt: Report examines how the U.S. Treasury loses $35B annually through tax preferences for domestic fossil fuels, including intangible drilling costs and percentage depletion.

title: Ways and Means Approves Proposed TCJA Extensions and Tax Changes
excerpt: The Ways and Means Committee approved sweeping TCJA extensions, making lower individual rates, enhanced deductions, and the 20% pass-through deduction permanent.

title: PAC Industry Breakdown: Where the Money Goes
excerpt: OpenSecrets tracks which industries are spending the most through PACs, with finance, real estate, and healthcare consistently topping the list of contributors to federal candidates.

title: The New Cost for 2025 Tax Cut Extensions: $4 Trillion
excerpt: Analysis of the $4T price tag for extending TCJA provisions, the centerpiece of lobbying efforts by industry groups with PACs funding tax-writing committee members.

title: Which Industries Are Giving the Most Money to PACs and Candidates?
excerpt: Overview of which sectors funnel the most money through PACs and direct contributions, revealing the scale of industry investment in influencing tax policy.
